title: Hymiskviða
stanzas: Thor the mighty set forth one day,
With Tyr by his side as a companion true,
To seek the hall of Hymir the giant old,
Who dwelt at the edge of the world so wide.
They came at last to his dwelling great,
And there they found his wife so wise,
She bid them welcome and gave them food,
While Hymir himself was out at sea.
When the giant returned with a catch of fish,
He saw the two gods in his dwelling place,
And he grew angry with bitter words,
'Why have ye come to disturb my peace?'
'We have come to test thy strength," said Thor,
'And to fish in the depths of the sea so wide,
We need a vessel in which to sail,
And bait to catch the fish below.'
Hymir gave them a cauldron vast,
So large that a man could scarce contain,
Its depths and breadth in his arms spread wide,
Then he sailed with them to the sea.
Thor prepared his fishing line with care,
And baited it with the head of an ox,
Then he cast it out in the depths below,
And waited for fish to take the bait.
At last the line grew taut and tight,
And Thor pulled up with all his might,
The Midgard serpent from out of the sea,
That lies in the waves and coils around the earth.
The serpent rose with a roar so loud,
That the waves did crash on the sea and sky,
And Thor raised Mjolnir to strike it dead,
But the line did break and it sank below.
Then Hymir grew pale with fear and dread,
For he knew that Thor had waged great war,
With the serpent that lay in the ocean deep,
'Let us return to the shore,' he cried.
And Thor agreed, for the battle was done,
Though he had not slain the great serpent there,
But the fire of his wrath had been kindled bright,
And he vowed that one day he would finish the fight.
